title: DB3 pipe chart, no auto-jump, SaaS plan PDF upload
description:
Three fixes for PipeShare / PipeSync reliability.

DB3 / pipe chart: folder DB3 links now load under the portal job you are viewing (Customer or Employee), not a Wasabi tree-inferred job that could wipe Customer bindings after soft-switch. Admins get all inspection bindings for the job; empty server responses no longer erase a just-saved local link.

Billable package: after a successful copy you stay on the Employee (source) folder — no auto-switch to Customer and no sticky reopen of that package path.

SaaS Plan View: tenant host binding accepts hyphen-insensitive subdomain vs storage slug (techpipe vs tech-pipe), PDF content-type is inferred when the browser sends octet-stream, and failed plan saves show an error instead of failing silently.

Who is affected: PipeShare on BASE; PipeSync Plan View on SaaS (after Push).
changeLog: Load DB3 bindings from portal UI job so Customer pipe chart keeps its links.
Stop auto-switching to Customer folder after billable package copy.
SaaS tenant host binding matches compact subdomain to hyphenated storage slug.
Plan View PDF upload: infer application/pdf; surface persist failures.
